      Today

    1. JPMorgan CEO flags Iran war-fueled inflation, private credit, and AI-linked job losses as risks to the economy

    2. 'Wouldn't be shocked' if bull market continues: Wall Street aims to look past Iran conflict

    3. What if I can’t pay my taxes? 5 ways to tackle your bill.

    4. The US labor market right now can be defined by one word: Whiplash

    5. The Trump administration has signaled it will offer broad tariff refunds. That could mean millions for companies.

    6. Inflation readings, oil volatility, and airline earnings: What to watch this week

    7. Britain's billion-dollar bet on a hypercar is "a genuinely incredible machine to drive," writes senior reporter Pras Subramanian.

    8. Cocoa prices are falling — but not in time for Easter (CNN Business)

    9. 7 things to know about Schwab's new brokerage account for teenagers

    10. The war in Iran shows no signs of slowing down, and its damage may be felt for years to come

    11. The best markets for first-time homebuyers are increasingly in these two regions of the US

    12. The company's gravitational pull has the potential to elevate Elon Musk as a government partner, Wall Street fixture, and tech entrepreneur.

    13. US judge upholds block of subpoenas in criminal investigation of Jay Powell (Financial Times)

    14. Hyundai's Boulder concept previews the automaker's truck future, built in America

    15. What we've learned in a year since 'Liberation Day'

    16. Americans nearing retirement plan to take Social Security 'as soon as eligible'

    17. March jobs report: US economy adds 178,000 jobs, unemployment rate falls to 4.3% in surprise turnaround

    18. The March jobs report will show a fuller picture of the state of the job market, just as the Iran war began to weigh on the global economy.

    19. Jet fuel prices are up 100%, oil prices keep surging, and airlines are paying the price

    20. Microsoft is building its own high-powered AI models, a sign that it is looking to wean itself from OpenAI

    21. Stocks shook off early losses, oil held on to gains as markets assessed a potential easing in the Strait of Hormuz

    22. Mortgage rates rose above 6% for the 5th straight week

    23. Stocks spiked after Iran announced it's working with Oman on a Strait of Hormuz traffic protocol

    24. US stocks fall at the open while oil surges after Trump says the war in Iran is not yet over

    25. What's changed (and what hasn't) in the year since Trump's 'Liberation Day'

    26. Watch live: President Trump addresses nation as Iran war rattles markets

    27. Nike set the stage for a turnaround, but global events keep getting in its way

    28. Bank of America rips up its economic forecasts as it braces for $100 oil all year on Iran war disruptions

    29. Trump says the Strait of Hormuz must be 'open, free and clear' before having ceasefire talks with Iran

    30. The Dow, S&P 500, and Nasdaq extended their rally for a second day ahead of Trump’s speech on Iran

    31. The Nasdaq led the Dow and S&P 500 higher on growing optimism the war in Iran may end soon

    32. SpaceX has confidentially filed for an initial public offering. SpaceX was most recently valued at $1.25 trillion.

    33. US stocks rise at the open as deescalation talk in Iran extends rally

    34. The bond market is helping resolve the Fed's interest rate dilemma

    35. Whoop CEO has eyes on IPO after company raises $575 million in funding at a $10.1 billion valuation

    36. Gold prices rallied on hopes of a swift end to the Iran war, but it still posted its worst month in over a decade

    37. Here are the highlights, lowlights, and everything else from the first quarter of 2026

    38. US stocks soared on hopes of a swift end to the Iran war, capping a brutal quarter on a high note

    39. The S&P 500 is up 2% and the Nasdaq is surging over 3% amid easing Iran war tensions.

    40. Hiring in the US hasn't been this slow since the depths of the pandemic

    41. US stocks rise as Trump indicates flexibility on the Strait of Hormuz

    42. TSA officers are receiving missed paychecks, which were at over $1 billion dollars in total

    43. US gas prices top $4 for the first time since August 2022

    44. The S&P 500 and Nasdaq fell for the third straight session as US oil closed above $100 for the first time since 2022.

    45. Fed Chair Powell sees no threat of private credit 'contagion,' says interest rates are in a 'good place'

    46. US stocks rise ahead of shortened week and jobs report

          Apple Inc. (AAPL)

          Time to act on AAPL?
          255.92 +0.29 (+0.11%)

          News headlines Apple continues to showcase strong profitability metrics, with a 32.4% operating margin and a robust future growth outlook. However, regulatory challenges in China and potential impacts from Globalstar's acquisition talks with Amazon may pose risks to its operations and market position.

          Apple continues to showcase strong profitability metrics, with a 32.4% operating margin and a robust future growth outlook. However, regulatory challenges in China and potential impacts from Globalstar's acquisition talks with Amazon may pose risks to its operations and market position.

          Updated 16m ago · Powered by Yahoo Scout
          • Previous Close 255.63
          • Open 254.14
          • Bid 241.35 x 100
          • Ask 266.91 x 100
          • Day's Range 250.65 - 256.13
          • 52 Week Range 169.21 - 288.62
          • Volume 31,289,369
          • Avg. Volume 47,741,423
          • Market Cap (intraday) 3.761T
          • Beta (5Y Monthly) 1.11
          • PE Ratio (TTM) 32.35
          • EPS (TTM) 7.91
          • Earnings Date Apr 30, 2026
          • Forward Dividend & Yield 1.04 (0.41%)
          • Ex-Dividend Date Feb 9, 2026
          • 1y Target Est 295.07

          Apple Inc. designs, manufactures, and markets smartphones, personal computers, tablets, wearables, and accessories worldwide. The company offers iPhone, a line of smartphones; Mac, a line of personal computers; iPad, a line of multi-purpose tablets; and wearables, home, and accessories comprising AirPods, Apple Vision Pro, Apple TV, Apple Watch, Beats products, and HomePod, as well as Apple branded and third-party accessories. It also provides AppleCare support and cloud services; and operates various platforms, including the App Store that allow customers to discover and download applications and digital content, such as books, music, video, games, and podcasts, as well as advertising services include third-party licensing arrangements and its own advertising platforms. In addition, the company offers various subscription-based services, such as Apple Arcade, a game subscription service; Apple Fitness+, a personalized fitness service; Apple Music, which offers users a curated listening experience with on-demand radio stations; Apple News+, a subscription news and magazine service; Apple TV, which offers exclusive original content and live sports; Apple Card, a co-branded credit card; and Apple Pay, a cashless payment service, as well as licenses its intellectual property. The company serves consumers, and small and mid-sized businesses; and the education, enterprise, and government markets. It distributes third-party applications for its products through the App Store. The company also sells its products through its retail and online stores, and direct sales force; and third-party cellular network carriers and resellers. The company was formerly known as Apple Computer, Inc. and changed its name to Apple Inc. in January 2007. Apple Inc. was founded in 1976 and is headquartered in Cupertino, California.

          www.apple.com

          Apple's 50 years of innovation, and how it has remained relevant

          D.A.
